item.title: Curl cheatsheet
item.description: Curl is an invaluable tool for anyone who needs to debug webapps fequently. Given it’s vast amount of options, it is easy to miss some of it’s simplest yet powerful uses. Let me add a few here, and I’ll keep updating it as and when I discover something new! 🤘 Inspect response headers The simplest way to do that is by using the -I option - $ curl -I https://httpbin.org/ip HTTP/2 200 date: Thu, 12 Nov 2019 19:08:13 GMT content-type: application/json content-length: 32 server: gunicorn/19.

item.title: About
item.description: A quick snapshot. Please drop me a note if you would like to discuss anything. Skills 💻 Amazon Web Services (AWS) Extensive working experience with AWS services like VPC, Route 53, EC2, EBS, ECS, EKS, ECR, API Gateway, Lambda, S3, Glacier, RDS, DynamoDB, EMR, Data Pipeline, SNS, SES, IAM, CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Trusted Advisor etc. Continuous Integration & Deployment (CI/CD) GIT, AWS Code Pipeline, Code Build, Code Deploy, Jenkins, Jenkins Pipelines and integrating them with a variety of different tools for automated tests, code quality checks, reporting etc.
